Purpose: Characterizing hierarchical population structure
Specifics: Arlequin allows for a variety of other analyses of diversity
Issues and warnings: Requires a priori assignment of individuals to populations, data formatting is required prior analysis

Purpose: Grouping individuals in spatially consistent clusters maximizing HW equilibrium
Specifics: Takes into account spatial variation, supposed to detect weak structure, framed in R
Issues and warnings: Immigrant alleles are assumed to be found only in new immigrants

Purpose: Grouping individuals in clusters maximizing HW equilibrium and LD between loci
Specifics: Fast (30X than ADMIXTURE)
Issues and warnings: Still slow computation time for very large datasets

Purpose: Grouping individuals in clusters maximizing HW equilibrium and LD between loci
Specifics: User friendly interface. Bayesian inference.
Issues and warnings: Not suited for whole genomes. Requires specific input format. Might be used on a small set of high quality markers for small genomes.

Purpose: Grouping individuals in clusters maximizing HW equilibrium and LD between loci
Specifics: ~100X faster than Structure
Issues and warnings: Approximate inference of the original Structure model

Purpose: Grouping individuals in clusters maximizing HW equilibrium and LD between loci
Specifics: Maximum Likelihood, claimed to be faster than Structure. Can handle sex-linked markers
Issues and warnings: Often slower than its counterparts

Purpose: Chromosome painting, admixture and clustering
Specifics: Estimates time since admixture, fast, set of scripts to facilitate analysis
Issues and warnings: Relies on Structure and fastStructure assumptions. Requires phased data.

Purpose: Chromosome painting
Specifics: Fast, uses HMM to smooth out windows and limit noise due to low confidence ancestry
Issues and warnings: Requires a priori definition of ancestral populations and phased haplotypes

Purpose: Chromosome painting, estimating admixture time and proportions.
Specifics: Can handle several source populations. These populations do not have to be good surrogates of populations that actually mixed.
Issues and warnings: Requires phased data, but performs phasing error correction.

Purpose: Summarizing variance across loci and visualizing inter-individual genetic distance
Specifics: Uses latent factors to correct for drift and position ancient samples in a PCA-like framework.
Issues and warnings: NA

Purpose: Grouping individuals in clusters maximizing HW equilibrium and LD between loci
Specifics: This method explicitly takes into account the age of samples. Useful when analyzing mixtures of modern and ancient samples.
Issues and warnings: Requires a genotype matrix in the eigenstrat format. Primarily tested on human data.

Purpose: Identifies contribution of environment and geographical distance to populations differentiation
Specifics: Less biased than Mantel tests, provides tools for model testing
Issues and warnings: Uses population-level data.

Purpose: Extracting summary statistics from pooled data
Specifics: Explicitely corrects for sampling bias in pooled data. Allows computing tests using an outgroup (MK test, HKA test, Fay and Wu's H) and characterizing coding mutations.
Issues and warnings: Mostly limited to summary statistics, but more complete than Popoolation.

Purpose: Extracting summary statistics from pooled data
Specifics: Explicitely corrects for sampling bias in pooled data. Can be used to detect TE polymorphisms.
Issues and warnings: Mostly limited to a few summary statistics. A pipeline dedicated to TE detection is also available
Reference: (Kofler, Orozco-terWengel, et al., 2011; Kofler, Pandey, et al., 2011)

Purpose: Computing summary statistics based on AFS and LD along genomes
Specifics: Accepts VCF and GFF/GFT files, efficient and fast. Tests for admixture available (ABBA BABA test). Includes basic coalescence simulations (ms and msms)
Issues and warnings: Mostly limited to summary statistics (but coalescent simulations are possible). No built-in SNP calling module

Purpose: Computing summary statistics based on AFS and LD along genomes
Specifics: Able to process BAM files, built-in procedures for data filtering, admixture analysis. Suited for low-depth data. Includes a suite of methods to estimate relatedness (NGSRelate).
Issues and warnings: Mostly limited to summary statistics. Tutorials not always up-to-date.

Purpose: Computing summary statistics based on AFS and LD along genomes
Specifics: Can output AFS for further analysis in fastsimcoal2
Issues and warnings: Slower than PopGenome, requires a specific format and file conversion.

Purpose: Computing summary statistics based on AFS and LD along genomes
Specifics: Fast. VCFTOOLS can also be used for SNP filtering
Issues and warnings: Less summary statistics than POPGenome

Purpose: Low depth sequencing/ancient samples analsis
Specifics: Particularly suited for analyzing ancient samples. Includes sets of tools to call variants, estimate post-mortem damage, inbreeding, genetic diversity. Produces the input file for PSMC (demography from a single diploid genome)
Issues and warnings: Better used in combination with GATK pipelines. Still in development

Purpose: Visualizing a matrix of pairwise differentiation statistics as a tree
Specifics: Can be used for pooled datasets, several statistics can be used
Issues and warnings: Differentiation measures alone do not necessarily retrieve the actual history of populations

Purpose: Estimating barriers to gene flow in a spatial context
Specifics: Estimates pairwise relatedness between all samples, and compares it to isolation-by-distance expectations to identify barriers to gene flow and corridors of higher connectivity. Can handle both haploid and diploid data
Issues and warnings: Requires to convert VCF file into PLINK binary format. Estimates effective migration rates (does not disantangle migration rates and effective population sizes). Setting parameters for the MCMC chain requires some trial-and-error

Purpose: Estimating barriers to gene flow in a spatio-temporal context
Specifics: Expands on EEMS, but takes into account the phase to reconstruct past changes in connectivity. Can disentantle migration rates and effective population sizes (unlike EEMS)
Issues and warnings: Relies on identity-by-descent tracks, requiring phasing (for example using BEAGLE). A pipeline to obtain IBD tracks is available, with a few details here: https://github.com/halasadi/ibd_data_pipeline/issues/1

Purpose: Grouping individuals in clusters maximizing HW equilibrium and LD between loci
Specifics: Incorporates geographic information of samples. Can run genome scans of selection based on contrasting ancestral and modern allele frequencies.
Issues and warnings: Importing data requires using conversion tools found in the LEA suite

Purpose: Summarizing variance across loci and visualizing inter-individual genetic distance
Specifics: Fast. Can use VCF files as an input
Issues and warnings: Requires careful interpretation (Jombard et al. 2009)

Purpose: Summarizing variance across loci and visualizing inter-individual genetic distance
Specifics: Fast. Can use VCF files as an input
Issues and warnings: Requires careful interpretation (Jombard et al. 2009)

Purpose: Maximizes divergence between groups identified by PCA
Specifics: Fast. Less sensitive to HWE assumptions. Claims to be more efficient than Structure
Issues and warnings: Requires careful interpretation (Jombard et al. 2009)

Purpose: Spatially explicit model to assess population structure
Specifics: Spatially explicit and able to detect cryptic structure. Fast.
Issues and warnings: Does not take into account HW equilibrium or LD

Purpose: Chromosome painting, relatedness
Specifics: LAMP also allows for association and pedigree analyses
Issues and warnings: Identifies local ancestry in windows (source of noise), requires phased data

Purpose: Estimating inbreeding and relatedness
Specifics: Allows studying identity by descent and by state. PLINK is a multi-purpose tool, facilitating data analysis within the same software
Issues and warnings: NA

Purpose: Estimating inbreeding and relatedness
Specifics: Computes unadjusted Ajk and kinship coefficient
Issues and warnings: NA

Purpose: Estimating inbreeding and relatedness, multivariate analysis
Specifics: Mendelian error checking, testing family structure, highly accurate kinship coefficient, association analysis, population structure inference
Issues and warnings: Kinship coefficient also computed in VCFTOOLS

Purpose: Pedigree inference from SNPs
Specifics: Robust even with high error rates (e.g. low-depth sequencing). Can handle haplo-diploids systems (e.g. ants). Multi-threaded.
Issues and warnings: Can only simulate genotypes with the Windows version.

Purpose: Pedigree inference from SNPs (modern panels included)
Specifics: R package. Routinely applied to whole-genome SNP datasets after LD-pruning down to a few thousand markers. Scales to >1000 individuals, accommodates unknown birth times and partial pedigrees. Produces uncertainty estimates and is widely used in conservation and animal-breeding contexts.
Issues and warnings: Best with LD-pruned, high-quality SNPs (~1k-5k after pruning is common). Heavy missing data and miscalled genotypes inflate false relatedness. Sex and approximate age priors substantially help.

Purpose: Estimating variation in recombination rates along a genome
Specifics: Handles unphased and missing data, underlying model can be used for organisms such as viruses or bacteria
Issues and warnings: Limited to 300 sequences, specific format (not VCF), model for recombination hotspots based on human data

Purpose: Identifying recombination hotspots
Specifics: Specifically designed for detecting recombination hotspots
Issues and warnings: Requires data to be phased, working with LDHat

Purpose: Recombination from a single diploid genome
Specifics: No phasing needed. Accepts VCF files as input.
Issues and warnings: Introgression and demographic misspecification may bias results. No detailed tutorial

Purpose: Estimating variation in recombination rates along a genome
Specifics: Higher accuracy than LDHat
Issues and warnings: Requires phased data. Does not handle VCF, only fasta and fastq formats. Requires dividing the genome in short segments to be analysed in parallel.

Class of method: F-statistics / admixture testing (Pool-Seq and allele counts)
Purpose: Compute Patterson F-, f- and D-statistics, fit and search admixture graphs, from Pool-Seq read counts or individual allele counts
Specifics: R package. Implements unbiased estimators for both individual allele-count and Pool-Seq data under a unified probability-of-identity framework. Supports three-population test of admixture, four-population test of treeness, F4-ratio admixture-rate estimation, and (semi-automatic) admixture-graph construction and visualisation. Reads VCFs, BayPass / TreeMix / SNP-matrix formats directly. The natural pairing with BayPass for Pool-Seq workflows.
Issues and warnings: Pool-Seq estimators require correct pool sizes and pool coverages; check that the right input class (pooldata vs. countdata) is used. Graph-search heuristics can return multiple equally-fit topologies — interpret cautiously, ideally with bootstrapped F-statistics.

Purpose: Pairwise relatedness inference from genotype likelihoods (low-coverage NGS)
Specifics: Estimates relatedness directly from genotype likelihoods, so works on low-coverage data where genotype callers fail. ngsRelateV2 estimates the full set of nine condensed Jacquard coefficients, accommodating inbred individuals. Works directly from BAM/CRAM or VCF/BCF. Threaded; scales linearly with cores.
Issues and warnings: Allele-frequency mis-specification (especially for inbred or admixed populations) biases coefficients. Below ~1× coverage, accuracy drops sharply for distant relatives - cross-check with READv2 / TKGWV2 / KIN or with PMR-based methods.

Purpose: Relatedness inference robust to unknown population structure
Specifics: R/Bioconductor package (GENESIS). Models structure with PCs and estimates kinship coefficients and IBD-sharing probabilities without requiring a panmictic reference. Standard in human GWAS pipelines and the recommended choice when populations are admixed or structured. Adopted in aDNA workflows for the same reason.
Issues and warnings: Requires accurate PC inference upstream (filter related individuals first or use KING-robust to seed). Performance depends on the number of PCs included; check sensitivity.

Purpose: Phasing and imputation of SNP genotypes
Specifics: Long-running standard for haplotype phasing and reference-based imputation. Beagle 5.x is fast and has good biobank-scale performance. Output of phasing feeds into hap-ibd, IBDNe / HapNe-IBD, rehh, selscan, and any haplotype-based downstream tool.
Issues and warnings: Phasing accuracy degrades for small samples without a reference panel - simulate switch errors at your sample size to gauge bias on downstream IBD/Ne estimates.

Purpose: Statistical phasing at biobank scale
Specifics: Designed for hundreds of thousands of phased samples; explicit handling of rare variants, which earlier phasers struggled with. Now the standard for UK Biobank-scale popgen.
Issues and warnings: Recombination-map dependence; performance on non-human species depends on reference availability.

Purpose: Read-based phasing using long reads or read pairs
Specifics: Default tool for read-based phasing of long-read or paired-end short-read data, especially when no reference panel is available. Often used in non-model species and as the first step before a population-based phasing tool.
Issues and warnings: Read-based phasing range is limited by read/insert length; combine with population-based phasing (Beagle / SHAPEIT5) to extend phase blocks.

Purpose: VCF/BCF manipulation, filtering, normalisation, statistics
Specifics: The standard command-line toolkit for VCF processing - filtering, normalising multi-allelics (`bcftools norm`), splitting and merging samples, computing per-site stats. A near-universal upstream dependency of every tool in these tables.
Issues and warnings: Multi-allelic and complex variants need careful normalisation before downstream popgen statistics — silent biases otherwise.

Purpose: Compute and plot genome-wide LD decay from VCFs
Specifics: Lightweight, fast tool used in non-model species to summarise LD decay - useful for choosing windows for selection scans, estimating Ne from r2, and for sanity-checking phasing.
Issues and warnings: Decay curves are sensitive to MAF filters; report the threshold used.

Purpose: Hierarchical F-statistics, gene diversity, and basic popgen summaries from individual SNP data
Specifics: R package for classical hierarchical Wright-style F-statistics with bootstrapping. Useful baseline alongside StAMPP / adegenet for population-genomic summaries.
Issues and warnings: Designed for individual genotypes, not Pool-Seq.

Purpose: Population structure (sNMF) and environmental association (LFMM) in a single R package
Specifics: R/Bioconductor package providing sNMF (a fast STRUCTURE-like algorithm) and LFMM2 for genotype-environment association. Often used as the R-native counterpart to ADMIXTURE + BayPass.
Issues and warnings: sNMF and LFMM both assume continuous ancestry; for highly structured datasets, complement with conStruct / ADMIXTURE.

Purpose: Genotype-environment association (GEA) via redundancy analysis
Specifics: The classical multivariate GEA workflow: rda() in the R vegan package on individual-level genotype matrices, with environmental predictors as constraining variables. Partial RDA (pRDA) conditions out geography or population structure. Outliers are loci with extreme loadings on the constrained axes. Forester et al. (2018) Mol Ecol established the now-standard pipeline.
Issues and warnings: Linear model — does not capture non-linear environment-genotype dependencies (use RDAforest for that). Sensitive to imputation of missing genotypes and to collinearity among predictors. Outlier thresholds are heuristic; bootstrap or empirical-null calibration is recommended.

Purpose: Phasing and genotype imputation from low-coverage sequencing or aDNA
Specifics: Iterative Gibbs sampler that refines genotype likelihoods against a haplotype reference panel. GLIMPSE2 scales to biobank-scale reference panels and is now the standard low-coverage workflow, including for ancient DNA at coverages as low as 0.1×. Output drops directly into popgen pipelines (PCA, ADMIXTURE, IBD).
Issues and warnings: Imputation accuracy is strongly dependent on reference-panel ancestry match. Post-imputation filtering on INFO score / GP / DS is essential before downstream popgen analyses, especially for rare variants. PCA on raw imputed dosages is biased - use GDI-style filters first.

Purpose: Non-linear PCA for low-coverage sequencing data
Specifics: Maximally correlated PCA on genotype dosages; better explains variance and resolves close populations than standard PCA when coverage is low.
Issues and warnings: Newer than PCAngsd; benchmarking on non-human data is limited.

Purpose: Reference-panel-free genotype imputation from low-coverage sequencing
Specifics: HMM that models each chromosome as a mosaic of K unknown founder haplotypes inferred jointly from the sample itself - so no haplotype reference panel is required. The default tool for non-model species, livestock, mice, and any taxon without a curated reference panel. Robust at coverages as low as 0.15x given enough samples; widely used in conservation and quantitative-genetics studies.
Issues and warnings: Needs a high-quality assembly for read mapping and a list of candidate variant positions. Accuracy depends on sample size (more samples = better founder-haplotype estimation) and on the chosen K; tune both. For populations with high relatedness/inbreeding, accuracy is very high; for low-relatedness populations recent benchmarks show meaningful differences vs. panel-based tools (Wang et al. 2025).

Purpose: Fast genotype imputation from low-coverage sequencing using a reference panel
Specifics: Models reads as carrying maternal/paternal labels, enabling per-chromosome imputation with linear scaling in panel size. Companion to STITCH from the same lab - use QUILT when you have a large reference panel (e.g. UK Biobank, 1000G) and STITCH when you don't. QUILT-HLA performs HLA-specific imputation.
Issues and warnings: Reference-panel ancestry match remains the main accuracy driver. Post-imputation INFO/GP/DS filters needed before popgen analyses.

Purpose: Identify environmental drivers of polygenic adaptation
Specifics: Random-forest extension of redundancy analysis (RDA): explains the genetic covariance structure with multiple environmental predictors, captures non-linear / non-monotonic effects and interactions, includes adaptive-neighbourhood mapping. R package.
Issues and warnings: Does not directly identify causal SNPs; designed for polygenic architectures. Predictor collinearity must be handled (a built-in selection routine is provided).

Purpose: Local ancestry inference along chromosomes from phased data
Specifics: Discriminative random-forest classifier; v2 is faster and more accurate at biobank scale. Standard for human two- and three-way admixture studies.
Issues and warnings: Requires phased data and a curated reference panel; performance degrades for closely related references and very old admixture.

Purpose: Local ancestry inference along chromosomes
Specifics: Two-layer HMM that does not require phasing or a recombination map. Robust to ancient admixture (>=100 generations).
Issues and warnings: Slower than RFMix2 at biobank scale; assumes a fixed number of ancestral populations.

Purpose: Local ancestry inference, including for non-human species
Specifics: Parameter-light copying-model; works well in non-model organisms where reference panels are imperfect.
Issues and warnings: Requires phased data; calibration of switch error vs. ancestry change can be tricky.

Purpose: Local ancestry inference with calibrated probabilities
Specifics: XGBoost base learners + smoother CRF; very accurate at biobank scale and provides calibrated probabilities, exposed in the All of Us / 23andMe stack.
Issues and warnings: Needs reference panel matched to query populations and phased data.

Purpose: Biobank-scale local ancestry inference
Specifics: HMM with efficient implementation, scales to hundreds of thousands of phased genomes.
Issues and warnings: Phased data required; reference-panel choice strongly impacts accuracy.

Purpose: f-statistic toolkit (qpAdm, qpGraph, qp3Pop, f4) with automated graph search
Specifics: R/Rcpp reimplementation of ADMIXTOOLS; orders of magnitude faster, supports automated qpGraph topology search and bootstrap.
Issues and warnings: Inherits classical caveats (rotation tests, reference choice, sensitivity to drift assumptions). Graph search can find equally good but topologically different fits.

Purpose: Heuristic search of admixture graph topologies
Specifics: Wrapper around qpGraph that explores graph space and returns best-fitting topologies, often used in archaeogenetics.
Issues and warnings: Depends on classical qpGraph; computationally heavy for many populations.

Purpose: R interface for ADMIXTOOLS analyses (qpAdm/qpGraph/qpDstat)
Specifics: Implements the qpAdm 'rotating' protocol (Harney et al. 2021) for systematic source/reference selection.
Issues and warnings: Requires EIGENSTRAT-formatted data; rotation strategy can produce many plausible models that need evaluation.

Purpose: Estimating effective migration surfaces from spatial genetic data
Specifics: Penalised likelihood reformulation of EEMS; orders of magnitude faster, no MCMC tuning.
Issues and warnings: Like EEMS, infers effective migration (does not separate Ne and m). Sensitive to choice of grid resolution and lambda penalty.

Purpose: Distinguish discrete vs. continuous (isolation-by-distance) population structure
Specifics: Spatial extension of STRUCTURE-like clustering; tests whether layers add over a continuous IBD background.
Issues and warnings: Computationally intensive (Stan/MCMC); spatial layers can be hard to interpret biologically.

Purpose: Spatial visualisation of population structure on a map without admixture assumptions
Specifics: Unsupervised projection-based method, alternative/complement to PCA + map overlays.
Issues and warnings: Visualisation tool — does not by itself test demographic hypotheses.

Purpose: Simulate admixed haplotypes and manipulate haplotype/local-ancestry data
Specifics: Python toolkit for simulating admixed cohorts (haptools simgenotype/simphenotype) and converting between LA file formats.
Issues and warnings: Simulation only; does not perform inference itself.

Purpose: Fast detection of long IBD and HBD segments
Specifics: Seed-and-extend algorithm; standard input for IBDNe / HapNe-IBD.
Issues and warnings: Phased data required; switch errors fragment IBD segments and bias downstream Ne estimates.

Purpose: Ultra-fast IBD detection for biobank-scale cohorts
Specifics: Locality-sensitive hashing; designed for >100k samples.
Issues and warnings: Less sensitive for short IBD segments; phased input required.

Purpose: IBD detection from unphased sequence data
Specifics: Useful when high-quality phasing is unavailable; runs on VCFs.
Issues and warnings: Less powerful for short IBD segments than phased methods.

Purpose: IBD detection in large cohorts
Specifics: Hash-based seed detection. Frequently used as input to IBDNe.
Issues and warnings: Sensitive to genotyping error and phasing quality.

Purpose: Joint inference of admixture proportions and population-specific allele frequencies, with a covariance model of population history
Specifics: Outputs allele-frequency covariance graph that can be used to test for selection; sometimes preferred to ADMIXTURE for more interpretable downstream use.
Issues and warnings: Slower than ADMIXTURE for many populations; mostly tested on humans.

Purpose: PCA, admixture proportions and selection scans from genotype likelihoods (low-coverage data)
Specifics: Works directly from BAM-derived genotype likelihoods (e.g. ANGSD output); now the standard for low-coverage popgen.
Issues and warnings: Sensitive to depth heterogeneity and reference allele bias; iterative imputation can mask signals when depth is very low.

Purpose: Diagnose model fit of ADMIXTURE/STRUCTURE-like results
Specifics: Computes the residual correlation of allele frequencies between pairs of individuals to flag mis-specified K or unmodelled structure.
Issues and warnings: Does not by itself recommend a 'correct' K — it only flags poor fit.

Purpose: Local-ancestry-aware GWAS in admixed populations
Specifics: Models effects on each ancestry segment separately; reduces inflation in admixed cohorts.
Issues and warnings: Requires accurate local-ancestry calls upstream; not a structure-detection tool itself.

Purpose: Visualisation, alignment and summarising of multiple ADMIXTURE/STRUCTURE Q-matrices
Specifics: Solves the cluster-label permutation problem across runs and Ks; standard companion of ADMIXTURE in human and non-model studies.
Issues and warnings: Visualisation only; does not change the underlying inference.
